goal:
Create a small but fully animated browser game, split into exactly 3 files: index.html, style.css, and script.js. Follow these specs:
Game Concept

A simple "Catch the Falling Stars" arcade game — the player controls a basket/paddle at the bottom of the screen using arrow keys or mouse movement, catching falling stars while avoiding falling bombs. Score increases per star caught; 3 missed/bomb hits ends the game.
File Structure & Responsibilities

index.html — minimal markup only: a <canvas> element, a score/lives HUD overlay, a start screen overlay, and a game-over overlay with a "Play Again" button. Links to style.css and script.js.
style.css — all visual styling: centered canvas with a nice border/shadow, gradient background, styled HUD text, animated overlay transitions (fade in/out for start and game-over screens), button hover effects, responsive sizing for different screen widths.
script.js — all game logic, organized into clear small functions/classes:

Player class (paddle: position, width, movement, draw method)
FallingObject class (shared base for stars and bombs: position, speed, draw method, off-screen check)
Spawner logic that creates new falling objects at randomized intervals and x-positions, with stars spawning more frequently than bombs
Collision detection function (rectangle/circle overlap between paddle and falling objects)
Game state management (start, playing, gameOver) controlling which overlay is visible
Score and lives tracking, increasing falling speed gradually as score increases (difficulty ramp)
Main gameLoop() using requestAnimationFrame, clearing and redrawing the canvas every frame
Input handling: keyboard arrow keys AND mouse/touch movement for paddle control
Particle burst effect (small animated circles/sparkles) when a star is caught, and a screen-shake or flash effect when a bomb is hit



Animation Requirements (all built with simple logic, no external libraries)

Stars rotate slowly and pulse in size while falling.
Bombs have a flickering fuse animation (a small flame/spark drawn above them, alternating color/size each frame).
Paddle has a subtle squash-and-stretch animation when catching an object.
Smooth particle burst (5–10 small circles expanding outward and fading) on every successful catch.
Background has slowly drifting decorative elements (e.g. faint twinkling stars or clouds) using simple sine-wave movement, purely for ambiance.
Start screen and game-over screen fade/slide in smoothly via CSS transitions, not JS animation.
Game-over screen displays final score with a small bounce-in animation.

Polish

Use requestAnimationFrame properly with delta-time so the game doesn't speed up/slow down on different machines.
Keep all code clean, commented, and organized into logical sections (Setup, Classes, Spawning, Update, Draw, Input, Game Loop).
No external assets or libraries — everything (shapes, stars, bombs, particles) is drawn with canvas primitives (arcs, paths, gradients) or simple CSS/emoji.
Make sure the game is fully playable, restartable, and bug-free (no overlapping event listeners on restart).
log_tail: Plan: Build a 3-file project (HTML, CSS, JS) implementing a paddle-based arcade game with particle effects, difficulty scaling, and state management. (3 steps)
Step 1/3: Create the HTML structure with canvas and UI overlays
Step 2/3: Create the CSS for layout, animations, and overlays
Step 3/3: Implement the full game logic in script.js
